title: Emit an audit record for every controlled action
description: Each action on a regulated resource writes an append-only record of who, what, when, and the before/after state.
body: Use when adding or changing any code path that creates, reads, modifies, or deletes a regulated resource (financial transaction, health record, access grant). Do not rely on application logs that can be edited or rotated away. Actions: write a structured audit record carrying actor identity, timestamp, action, resource id, and before/after values to an append-only sink at the point the action commits, inside the same transaction. Validation signals include a test that performing the action produces exactly one immutable record with all required fields. Risk boundary: a controlled action that can succeed without emitting an audit record is a blocking finding; escalate rather than ship the path.
